meta.title: Mozey — All Museums in One App
meta.description: Discover 281 museums and 101 historical places across Uzbekistan. Offline mode, 3 languages, dark mode, and much more.

hero.subtitle: A guide to the country's cultural heritage: from the Louvre in the desert to Registan. Read, save routes, and open the map — even where there's no signal.
hero.offlineBadge: The entire catalog works offline — articles, map, and photos are preloaded.

offline.title: Fully offline
offline.text: Every article, map, and photo is preloaded. Browse the catalog in Khiva without internet or roaming.

map.title: Map nearby
map.text: All 382 points on a single country map. Plan your day around nearby landmarks in one city.

bookmarks.title: Favorites & routes
bookmarks.text: Save museums and places to one list — it stays with you on the trip, no account needed.

audio.title: Audio guide in three languages
audio.text: Uzbek, Russian, and English for key sites — listen right on the spot.

catalog.title: Museums & historical places
catalog.emptyText: Try changing the query or region.

mapSection.title: The entire country — on one map
mapSection.subtitle: From Nukus in the west to Tashkent in the east. Click a point to open the site.
mapSection.nearbyPoints: {count} nearby points · opens in Google Maps

reviews.title: Traveling the country with the app

review1.text: Downloaded it before a trip to Samarkand — and never once pulled out a guidebook. Everything at hand, even without internet.
review1.name: Dinara

review2.text: Articles are short but to the point — in a minute you understand why a place matters. The map helped plan my day in Bukhara.
review2.name: Alexey

review3.text: Finally a decent app about Uzbekistan's museums. Saved a route, audio guide in Russian — exactly what I needed.
review3.name: Sabina

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Does the app really work offline?
faq.a1: Yes. After installation, the entire catalog — articles, map, and images — is stored on your device. No internet needed for reading or map navigation.
faq.q2: How much does the app cost?
faq.a2: The app is free. Prices shown on cards are the approximate admission fees to the museums and sites, in Uzbekistani som.
faq.q3: What languages is the content available in?
faq.a3: The interface and articles are in Uzbek, Russian, and English. Key sites also have an audio guide in these three languages.
faq.a4: No. Favorites and routes are stored locally on your device — no account or data transfer required.
faq.q5: How often is the catalog updated?
faq.a5: We regularly add new museums and landmarks and update opening hours. Updates arrive with new app versions.

cta.title: Take the heritage of Uzbekistan with you
cta.subtitle: 382 museums and landmarks, map and audio guide — in your pocket and without internet. Install the app before your trip.

footer.description: A guide to the museums and landmarks of Uzbekistan. Works offline.
